objective: Reviewed the plan to align CI, release workflow, and bump-version.sh with the new core/+cli/ Cargo workspace for correctness, completeness, and architecture alignment.
key_changes: All premises verified independently: virtual workspace (root no [package]), cli=grove bin, core=grove-core lib, both at 0.1.11 in Cargo.lock.
release.yml -p grove change is correct and defensive; asset path target/<t>/release/grove unchanged. cargo build --release --locked -p grove confirmed working.
ci.yml --workspace is future-proofing — bare cargo build/test already builds all members in the current virtual workspace; value is defensive against a future root [package].
bump-version.sh: cargo update -p grove -p grove-core is valid; path dep has no version constraint so lock picks up new version cleanly; idempotency holds.
All four verify-only files confirmed unchanged by the proposed edits.
Advisory: throwaway-version bump test should be required (not optional) to prove cargo update writes a NEW version into both lock entries.
Advisory: stale header comment and final echo message in bump-version.sh should be refreshed to reflect two-crate reality.
No security concerns; VERSION is regex-gated before perl interpolation. Stack checklist items all respected.
